description: Renamed plans to Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum (Toronto Profile)

steps.name: Context Awareness
steps.nina_response: Sure, I can help with that.
steps.follow_up: I found your profile — **John, 32, based in Toronto**. Just confirming, is this still correct?

steps.name: Light Personalization
steps.nina_response: Great. I just need a bit more context to narrow things down.
steps.follow_up: Are you married? And how would you describe your lifestyle — more active, or more social/travel-heavy?

steps.name: Set Expectation
steps.follow_up: I’ll shortlist a few plans that fit your profile, and then we can go deeper based on what matters most to you.

steps.name: Plan Comparison
steps.nina_response: I’ve narrowed this down to 4 plans that fit well for you. Here’s a quick view comparing coverage, premium, and key benefits.
steps.table:
| Plan | Monthly Premium | Coverage Limit | Key Benefits | Best For |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Bronze | $29 | $50K | Basic hospitalization | Budget |
| Silver | $59 | $100K | Specialist visits, health wallet | Balanced |
| Gold | $69 | $250K | Critical illness, higher limits | Families |
| Platinum | $129 | Unlimited | Zero co-pay, global coverage | Premium |
steps.follow_up:
At a high level:
• Bronze is basic
• Silver is balanced
• Gold adds stronger protection
• Platinum is premium, no compromises

Before I recommend — what matters more to you: lower monthly cost, or better long-term coverage?

steps.name: Recommendation
steps.nina_response: Silver is a solid choice — most users start there. But based on your profile — married and fairly active lifestyle — you’re slightly more exposed to health risks over time.
steps.follow_up:
For about $10 more per month, **Gold** gives you:
• 2.5x higher coverage
• Critical illness protection
• Better long-term flexibility

If you’re optimizing purely for cost → Silver works
If you’re optimizing for peace of mind → Gold is the better fit

steps.name: Exploration Platinum
steps.nina_response: Platinum is more of a premium option. It’s usually chosen by users who want maximum coverage and don’t want to worry about edge cases.
steps.follow_up: Think of it this way: Gold is strong and balanced, while Platinum is full coverage with fewer trade-offs. If budget isn’t a concern, Platinum is great. Otherwise, Gold is a very solid choice. Which one shall we go with?

steps.name: Quote Generation Permission
steps.nina_response: Would you like me to generate a quote for **Gold**?

steps.name: Action / Quote
steps.nina_response: Done! I’ve created your quote and sent it to your email.
steps.follow_up: You'll see a simple summary, key inclusions/exclusions, and a link to continue. You don't need long documents unless you want to.

steps.name: Close
steps.nina_response: No problem. I’m here whenever you need. Let me know if you’d like help comparing more options later!
steps.follow_up: Is there anything else I can assist with?
